etymology.description: 東(東) (dōng, "east") + 東(東) (dōng, "east") -> pair; peer, same kind -> 囗(囗) (wéi, "enclosure") added, later corrupted to 甘(甘) (gān, "sweet") and 曰(曰) (yuē, "to say") -> 曹(曹) (cáo, "office")
etymology.interpretation: 𣍘 is an intermediate form of 曹(曹) (cáo, "office"), born from the ancient character 㯥 representing a pair or peer of similar items. It showcases character evolution where additional and corrupted components marked development.
